schema_notes: These cases are history-derived candidates, not canonical benchmark entries yet.
They are intentionally llm-graded because the current bindings/oracle surface does not yet encode them cleanly.
Promote a candidate into evaluation/catalog.json only after adding stable oracle surfaces or agreeing that llm-only grading is acceptable for that task family.

cases.title: Normalize released JSON contracts coherently
cases.prompt: Normalize the released JSON/API surface to camelCase, keep discriminator field names on `type`, and use CAPS_CASE discriminator values consistently across the system. Update the code, fixtures, docs, and eval-facing examples together so the published contract stops drifting.
cases.expected_output: A coordinated contract reset that leaves the published JSON surface internally consistent.

expectations.text: Updates all affected released contract surfaces together rather than fixing only one serializer hotspot
Uses `type` for discriminators and applies CAPS_CASE discriminator values consistently
Keeps fixtures, examples, and docs aligned with the code changes so the repo does not publish contradictory contract shapes
Avoids unrelated behavior changes outside the contract normalization scope

cases.title: Thread richer declaration payloads through semantic reads
cases.prompt: Add a declaration payload that captures the full PSI text range and optional source text for declarations, then thread it through the API, both backends, and the CLI. Make it default-on for outline responses and opt-in for resolve/workspace-symbol body-heavy calls.
cases.expected_output: A repo-wide implementation of richer declaration payloads without breaking existing semantic read surfaces.

expectations.text: Threads the new declaration payload through the shared contract, both backends, and the CLI surface
Enables the payload by default on outline responses while keeping resolve/workspace-symbol body inclusion opt-in
Preserves wire compatibility expectations for omitted optional fields instead of forcing noisy null payloads
Avoids broadening unrelated read or mutation surfaces beyond the declaration-payload task

cases.title: Port shell wrappers into typed Kotlin CLI commands
cases.prompt: Rewrite the remaining shell-based Kast skill wrappers as typed Kotlin CLI commands inside `kast-cli`, wire them into the evaluation engine and CI, and keep the CLI contract honest so wrapper ergonomics stop drifting from the actual runtime surface.
cases.expected_output: A typed, test-backed wrapper surface in Kotlin with evaluation coverage and CI enforcement.

expectations.text: Ports the wrapper behavior into typed Kotlin command structures instead of leaving shell glue as the source of truth
Keeps CLI parsing and wrapper input contracts aligned with the actual runtime request shapes
Adds evaluation and CI backstops so wrapper drift is caught automatically instead of rediscovered by agents
Limits changes to the wrapper/eval/CI surfaces rather than turning the task into a broad CLI rewrite

source.summary: Derived from session 431e7b1e-b777-46e8-947f-ad90f0dfbc3c (Port skill wrappers to Kotlin).
